crates/arawn-auth/src/error.rs.name: AuthError
crates/arawn-auth/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Errors raised by the auth primitives.

crates/arawn-auth/src/lib.rs.name: error
crates/arawn-auth/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Provides a provider-agnostic OAuth2 client (`OAuthClient`), a local
single-shot HTTP callback listener (`CallbackServer`), and an
encrypted-at-rest token store (`TokenStore`). Together these implement
the full "one-time setup" flow: open a browser, wait for consent,
exchange code for token, persist it safely.

The crate is deliberately narrow — it owns the auth primitives and
nothing else. Higher-level integration/provider glue lives elsewhere.

crates/arawn-auth/src/oauth2.rs.doc_comment:
Generate a PKCE verifier + challenge + CSRF state and build the
authorization URL the user's browser should hit.

`redirect_uri` is the URL the callback server is listening on
(`http://127.0.0.1:<port>/<path>`). Pass the same value to
[`Self::exchange_code`].

crates/arawn-auth/src/token_store.rs.name: KEY_LEN
crates/arawn-auth/src/token_store.rs.doc_comment:
System spec's security contract and the sensitive-paths deny list.
The agent must not be able to read this directory — see the Facility

persisted with restrictive permissions. Subsequent opens load it.
On first use the master key is generated with cryptographic randomness and

```
<provider>.json.enc  — chacha20poly1305 ciphertext of the JSON token
.master.key          — 32-byte symmetric key, mode 0600
```text

Layout under `{data_dir}/tokens/`:

Encrypted on-disk storage for OAuth tokens.

A conversation session.
Scratch sessions start with `workstream_id = None`.
Once promoted to a workstream, the binding is immutable.

crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.name: new
crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.doc_comment: Create a session bound to a workstream.

crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.name: microcompact
crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.doc_comment:
Clear old tool results to save context space without an LLM call.
Replaces content of ToolResult messages older than `keep_recent` turns
for large-output tools (shell, file_read, grep, glob, web_fetch, web_search).
Returns total characters cleared.

crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.name: compact
crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.signature: (&mut self, summary_content: String, keep_recent: usize) -> usize
crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.doc_comment:
Replace old messages with a Summary, keeping the last `keep_recent` messages verbatim.
Returns the number of messages that were summarized.

crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.name: load_compacted
crates/arawn-core/src/session.rs.doc_comment:
Load messages with compaction awareness — if a Summary exists, use the
last Summary as the starting point and only include messages after it.

crates/arawn-engine/src/agent_defs.rs.name: AgentDefinition
crates/arawn-engine/src/agent_defs.rs.signature: { name: String, when_to_use: String, system_prompt: String, tools: Option<Vec<String>>, disallowed_tools: Option<Vec<Str...
crates/arawn-engine/src/agent_defs.rs.doc_comment: An agent definition — controls system prompt, tool access, and behavior.

crates/arawn-engine/src/agent_defs.rs.name: load_agents_dir
crates/arawn-engine/src/agent_defs.rs.doc_comment:
Load agent definitions from markdown files in a directory.

Each file should have YAML frontmatter with fields:
- `name` (required): agent type name
- `description` (required): when to use this agent
- `tools`: comma-separated list of allowed tool names, or "*" for all
- `disallowedTools`: comma-separated list of tools to exclude
- `model`: model override or "inherit"
- `maxTurns`: maximum agentic turns

The markdown body becomes the system prompt.

crates/arawn-engine/src/agent_defs.rs.name: get_all_agents

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.name: HookEvent
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.signature: PreToolUse | PostToolUse | PostToolUseFailure | PermissionRequest | PermissionDenied | SessionStart | SessionEnd | Setup...
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.doc_comment:
All 25 hook event types matching Claude Code's surface area.

Events for features not yet built (worktrees, subagents, etc.) are defined
but won't fire until those features land. This keeps the event surface
stable and forward-compatible.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.name: can_block
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.doc_comment: Whether this event can block execution (PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, UserPromptSubmit).

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.name: matcher_field
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.doc_comment: The field name that matchers filter on for this event type.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.name: summary
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.doc_comment: Human-readable summary of when this event fires.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.name: HookInput
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.signature: PreToolUse | PostToolUse | PostToolUseFailure | PermissionRequest | PermissionDenied | SessionStart | SessionEnd | Setup...
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.doc_comment:
Input data passed to hooks when they fire.

Each variant carries the fields relevant to its event type.
Serialized as JSON and piped to command hooks on stdin.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.name: event
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.doc_comment: Get the event type for this input.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.name: matcher_value
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/events.rs.doc_comment: Get the matcher field value for this input (the value that matchers filter on).

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/executor.r.name: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECS
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/executor.r.doc_comment: Default timeout for hook execution (10 seconds).

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/executor.r.name: CommandHookExecutor
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/executor.r.doc_comment:
Executes command hooks as shell subprocesses.

Pipes hook input as JSON on stdin, captures stdout/stderr,
and interprets exit codes:
- 0 → Allow (stdout suppressed by default)
- 2 → Block (stderr shown to model)
- Other → Warn (stderr shown to user, doesn't block)

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/executor.r.name: execute
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/executor.r.doc_comment:
Execute a command hook with the given input.

`cwd` is the working directory for the subprocess (typically the project root).

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/file_watch.name: HookFileWatcher
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/file_watch.doc_comment:
Watches file paths and fires `FileChanged` hooks when changes are detected.

Intended for project files that hooks care about (settings, config, etc.).
Uses debouncing to avoid firing on rapid successive changes.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/file_watch.name: spawn
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/file_watch.doc_comment: Spawn the file watcher as a background tokio task.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/loader.rs.name: SettingsFile
load_hooks_from_file
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/loader.rs.doc_comment:
Load hook configuration from a JSON settings file.

Returns an empty config if the file is missing, has no `hooks` key,
or contains malformed entries.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/loader.rs.name: load_merged_hooks
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/loader.rs.doc_comment:
Load and merge hook configs from user-level and project-level settings.

User settings (`~/.arawn/settings.json`) are merged with project settings
(`.arawn/settings.json`). Both sources' hooks are included — user hooks
run alongside project hooks. Deduplication by command+matcher collapses
identical hooks across sources.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/loader.rs.name: write_json
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/loader.rs.doc_comment: Helper to write raw bytes to a temp file (avoids write! macro brace escaping).

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/matcher.rs.name: HookMatcher
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/matcher.rs.doc_comment:
Matches hook events by a filterable field value (tool name, source, notification type, etc.)
and an optional content pattern.

# Matcher syntax

- `"Bash"` — exact match
- `"Bash|Edit"` — pipe-separated alternatives (any match)
- `"file_*"` — glob match (`*` = any chars, `?` = single char)
- `"Bash(git *)"` — tool name + content pattern
- `""` or absent — matches everything

Serializes to/from a plain JSON string (e.g. `"Bash"`, `"Edit(*.rs)"`).

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/matcher.rs.name: matches
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/matcher.rs.doc_comment:
Check if this matcher matches a given field value and optional content string.

`field_value` is the matchable field for the event type (e.g. tool_name, source).
`content` is the full tool input or other content for content-pattern matching.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/matcher.rs.name: glob_match
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/matcher.rs.doc_comment:
Simple glob matching supporting `*` (any chars) and `?` (single char).
Case-sensitive. No `**` or brace expansion.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/mod.rs.name: config
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/mod.rs.doc_comment:
The hooks system intercepts lifecycle events (tool execution, session
boundaries, permission prompts, compaction, etc.) and runs user-configured
shell commands. Hooks are configured in settings.json and matched by
event type + optional tool name / content patterns.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/mod.rs.doc_comment:

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/runner.rs.name: HookRunner
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/runner.rs.doc_comment:
Orchestrates hook matching, execution, and result aggregation.

Given a `HookConfig`, the runner:
1. Finds all hooks matching the event + field value
2. Executes them in parallel via `CommandHookExecutor`
3. Aggregates results (any Block → overall Block)

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/runner.rs.name: run
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/runner.rs.doc_comment: Run all matching hooks for the given input and return the aggregated result.

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/runner.rs.name: has_hooks
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/runner.rs.doc_comment: Check if any hooks are configured (useful for fast-path skipping).

crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/runner.rs.name: extract_content
crates/arawn-engine/src/hooks/runner.rs.doc_comment: Extract the content string used for content-pattern matching.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: PermissionMode
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Permission mode — controls fallback behavior when no explicit rule matches.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: fallback
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment:
Determine the fallback decision for a tool when no explicit rule
matched. Takes the tool's declared [`PermissionCategory`] plus the
tool name — the name is only used for the Plan-mode exception that
lets `enter_plan_mode` / `exit_plan_mode` through regardless of
category.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: PermissionResponse
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Response from a user when prompted for permission.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: ModalOption
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: A single option displayed in a modal prompt.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: ModalRequest
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.signature: { title: String, subtitle: Option<String>, options: Vec<ModalOption> }
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: A request to show a modal to the user and get a selection.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: ModalPrompt
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment:
Generic trait for prompting the user with a modal dialog.
Tools build their own ModalRequest and interpret the returned index.
Returns None if the user cancels (Escape), Some(index) if they select.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: SessionGrants
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment:
In-memory store for session-scoped permission grants.
When a user selects "Allow Always", the tool is added here
and won't be prompted again for the rest of the session.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: grant
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Record a session grant for a tool name.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: is_granted
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Check if a tool has been granted for this session.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: clear
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Clear all session grants.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: PermissionChecker
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.signature: { rules: std::sync::RwLock<Vec<PermissionRule>>, mode: std::sync::RwLock<PermissionMode>, grants: std::sync::Mutex<Sessi...
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment:
The central permission checker. Evaluates rules against tool calls,
prompts the user when needed, and manages session grants.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: new
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment:
Create a new permission checker with the given rules and default mode.
Without a prompter, `Ask` decisions are treated as `Denied`.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: with_mode
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Set the permission mode (Default, AcceptEdits, BypassPermissions).

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: with_prompter
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Set the modal prompter for interactive permission requests.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: update_rules
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Hot-reload: replace the current rules with new ones.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: update_mode
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Hot-reload: update the permission mode.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: check
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment:
Check if a tool call is permitted. The caller supplies the tool's
declared [`PermissionCategory`] — the engine looks this up via the
[`Tool`] trait (`tool.permission_category()`) before calling.

Flow:
1. Evaluate deny rules first (deny always wins, even over session grants)
2. Check session grants (short-circuit if granted and not denied)
3. Evaluate remaining rules: allow > ask > no match
4. If Ask, prompt the user (or deny if no prompter)
5. NoMatch falls through to permission mode fallback, which uses the
category to decide (ReadOnly auto-allow in Default, etc.)

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: prompt_user
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Prompt the user for permission (or deny if no prompter is configured).

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: mode
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Get the current permission mode.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: clear_grants
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Clear all session grants.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.name: MockPrompter
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/chec.doc_comment: Mock prompter that returns a fixed index (0=AllowOnce, 1=AllowAlways, 2/None=Deny).

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.name: PermissionConfig
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.signature: { allow: Vec<String>, deny: Vec<String>, ask: Vec<String> }
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.doc_comment:
Permission configuration — holds allow/deny/ask rule lists.

Loaded from settings and merged with priority (user > project > defaults).

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.name: into_rules
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.doc_comment:
Parse the string-based config into typed `PermissionRule` values.
Malformed entries are logged as warnings and skipped.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.name: merge
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.doc_comment:
Merge two configs: `self` is higher priority (e.g., user-level),
`other` is lower priority (e.g., project-level).

Higher-priority deny rules are prepended so they're checked first.
All rules from both sources are included.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.name: PermissionsSection
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.doc_comment:
Wrapper for the permissions section in the top-level config.
This gets embedded in ArawnConfig as an optional field.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.name: load_permissions_from_file
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.doc_comment:
Load permission config from a TOML file, returning defaults if the file
is missing or the permissions section is absent.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.name: load_merged_permissions
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/conf.doc_comment:
Load and merge permission configs from user-level and project-level files.

User config (`~/.arawn/arawn.toml`) takes priority over project config (`.arawn/arawn.toml`).

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/mod..name: checker
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/mod..doc_comment:
The permission system sits between the engine and tool execution, evaluating
rules against each tool call before it runs. Rules support tool name matching
(exact or glob) with optional content patterns.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/mod..name: config
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/mod..doc_comment:

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/prom.name: CliModalPrompt
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/prom.doc_comment:
CLI-based modal prompt. Prints options to stderr, reads selection from stdin.
Falls back to None (cancel) when stdin is not a TTY (non-interactive mode).

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/prom.name: MockModalPrompt
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/prom.doc_comment: Mock modal prompt for tests. Returns responses from a queue, or a default.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/prom.name: always
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/prom.doc_comment: Create a mock that always returns the given index.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/prom.name: with_responses
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/prom.doc_comment: Create a mock with queued responses.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.name: RuleKind
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.doc_comment: The kind of permission rule — what happens when it matches.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.name: PermissionRule
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.signature: { kind: RuleKind, tool_pattern: String, content_pattern: Option<String> }
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.doc_comment:
A single permission rule: a kind (allow/deny/ask), a tool name pattern,
and an optional content pattern for matching tool input.

# Format

Rules are specified as strings in settings:
- `"Read"` — exact tool name match
- `"file_*"` — glob match on tool name
- `"Bash(git *)"` — tool name + content pattern (matches Bash calls where input starts with "git")

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.name: parse
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.doc_comment: Parse a rule from the compact string format: `"ToolName"` or `"ToolName(content pattern)"`.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.name: matches
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.doc_comment: Check if this rule matches a given tool name and input.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.name: PermissionDecision
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.doc_comment: The result of evaluating permission rules against a tool call.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.name: RuleMatcher
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.doc_comment:
Evaluates a list of permission rules against a tool call.

Evaluation order: deny rules first (highest priority), then allow, then ask.
First match in each category wins. If no rules match, returns `NoMatch`.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.name: evaluate
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.doc_comment:
Evaluate rules against a tool call.

Priority: deny > allow > ask > no match.

crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.name: glob_match
crates/arawn-engine/src/permissions/rule.doc_comment:
Simple glob matching supporting `*` (any chars) and `?` (single char).
Case-sensitive. No `**` or brace expansion — keep it simple.

Install a plugin from a marketplace into the versioned cache.

Steps:
1. Look up marketplace in known_marketplaces.json
2. Fetch marketplace manifest, find the plugin

4. Register in installed_plugins.json

exist next to the manifest, use them. This matches Claude Code's behavior
where plugins don't need to explicitly list every component.

crates/arawn-engine/src/query_engine.rs.name: stream_response_with_retry
crates/arawn-engine/src/query_engine.rs.doc_comment:
Retry the request-build-and-stream cycle when the stream fails mid-flight.

This is a different retry layer from `arawn_llm::RetryClient`:
- `RetryClient` retries the `stream()` *open* call (connect-time
transient errors, e.g., 429 on initial HTTP request).
- This retry catches errors that surface *after* chunk consumption
has started (mid-stream network hiccups, provider closing the
stream with a transient error code). Those are invisible to
`RetryClient` — by the time it has handed back a stream, its
retry window is closed.

Rebuilds the full request on each attempt since session state is
unchanged and the previous stream's partial text is discarded.

Policy: 2 retries total (3 attempts), exponential backoff with
500 ms base (500 ms, 1 s) — shorter than `RetryClient`'s policy
because mid-stream is usually a transient hiccup and the caller
is already inside a user-facing turn.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.name: sensitive_deny_read_paths
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.doc_comment:
Build the list of sensitive paths that should be denied for reading.

Includes system auth files, SSH/GPG keys, cloud provider credentials,
container/package manager tokens, git credentials, database passwords,
shell history, and (on macOS) keychains/cookies.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.name: is_sensitive_path
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.doc_comment:
Returns true if `path` resolves into any sensitive directory.

Compares canonical forms when possible to defeat symlink and `..` traversal
tricks. Falls back to a lexical prefix check for non-existent paths.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.name: is_token_path
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.doc_comment:
Returns true if `path` resolves into the OAuth token directory under
`data_dir/tokens/`. Used by glob/grep/file_* tools to deny token reads
even when the user has put the data dir on `allowed_paths`. Symlink-aware
when both the input and the deny target exist.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.name: is_secret_file
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.doc_comment:
Returns true if the file at `path` matches a known secret-file pattern.

Matches by basename so `./app/.env` and `../../.env` are both blocked, while
legitimate files like `env.rs`, `.env.example`, and `environment.ts` are not.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.name: is_secret_filename
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sensitive_.doc_comment:
reject paths that resolve into any of these directories.
list to the OS sandbox; glob/grep/file tools call [`is_sensitive_path`] to
regardless of which tool is doing the reading. The shell sandbox passes this
Centralizes the set of paths that should never be readable by agent tools,

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: ShellTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a shell command within an OS-level sandbox.

Uses `sandbox-exec` (macOS) or `bubblewrap` (Linux) to enforce:
- Write access restricted to the session/workstream sandbox directory
- Sensitive paths denied for reading (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, credentials, etc.)
- Network blocked by default, unless the command invokes a known network tool

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: with_network_tools
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.doc_comment: Create a ShellTool with the given list of network-allowed tool binaries.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: with_background_manager
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.doc_comment: Attach a background task manager for `run_in_background` support.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: spawn_background
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.doc_comment:
Spawn a shell command as a background task. Returns immediately with the task ID.

Background commands run inside the same OS sandbox as foreground commands.
The sandbox manager is owned by the reader/waiter task and reset after the
child exits. If sandboxing is unavailable on this platform, the call fails
rather than silently running unsandboxed.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: init_sandbox_for_background
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.signature:
(
    command: &str,
    working_dir: &std::path::Path,
    network_tools: &[String],
) -> Result<(Arc<SandboxManager>, String), String>
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.doc_comment:
Initialize a sandbox manager for a background command and return it together
with the wrapped command string. The caller is responsible for eventually
calling [`SandboxManager::reset`] on the returned manager (usually after the
child process exits).

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: command_needs_network
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.doc_comment: Check if a command invokes any tool that needs network access.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: build_sandbox_config
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.doc_comment: Build a sandbox config for executing a command in the given working directory.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: execute
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.signature: (&self, ctx: &dyn arawn_tool::ToolContext, params: Value) -> Result<ToolOutput, ToolError>

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: execute_sandboxed
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.signature:
(
    command: &str,
    working_dir: &std::path::Path,
    timeout_ms: u64,
    network_tools: &[String],
) -> Result<ToolOutput, SandboxExecError>

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.name: execute_unsandboxed
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/shell.rs.signature:
(
    command: &str,
    working_dir: &std::path::Path,
    timeout_ms: u64,
) -> Result<ToolOutput, ToolError>

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/skill.rs.name: SkillTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Tool that executes skills (reusable prompt-based workflows).

The model calls this tool with a skill name and optional arguments.
The skill's prompt is returned as the tool output, which the model
then uses to guide its next response.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sleep.rs.name: SleepTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/sleep.rs.doc_comment:
Waits for a specified duration. Preferred over `Bash(sleep ...)` because
it doesn't hold a shell process.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..signature: { id: String, subject: String, description: Option<String>, active_form: Option<String>, status: TaskStatus }

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..name: SessionTaskStore
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..doc_comment:
Shared in-memory task store for a session.
Not persisted — lives only for the duration of the engine run.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..name: create
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..signature:

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..name: TaskCreateTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..doc_comment: Creates a new session-scoped task for tracking work within the current session.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..name: TaskUpdateTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..doc_comment: Updates a session task's status or details.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..name: TaskListTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..doc_comment: Lists all session tasks with their status.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..name: TaskGetTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_list..doc_comment: Gets full details of a session task by ID.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_outpu.name: TaskOutputTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/task_outpu.doc_comment:
Read the output and status of a background task.
Can block/poll until the task completes or return immediately.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/think.rs.name: ThinkTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/think.rs.doc_comment:
A no-op reasoning scratchpad tool.
The LLM uses this to think step-by-step without side effects.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..name: CacheEntry
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..signature: { content: String, content_type: String, fetched_at: Instant }

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..name: WebFetchTool
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..doc_comment:
Fetches content from a URL, converts HTML to markdown, caches results,
and optionally uses an LLM to extract information via the `prompt` parameter.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..name: execute
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..signature: (&self, ctx: &dyn arawn_tool::ToolContext, params: Value) -> Result<ToolOutput, ToolError>

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..name: process_content
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..doc_comment: Convert HTML to markdown, or return non-HTML as-is. Truncate to MAX_CONTENT_BYTES.

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..name: html_to_markdown
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..doc_comment: Convert HTML to markdown using htmd (Turndown-equivalent).

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..name: strip_html_tags
crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_fetch..doc_comment: Fallback: simple HTML tag stripper (used if htmd fails).

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_search.name: WebSearchTool

crates/arawn-engine/src/tools/web_search.signature: { title: String, url: String, snippet: String }

crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.name: AnthropicClient
crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.doc_comment: Client for Anthropic's Claude API (Messages API).

crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        request: ChatRequest,
    ) -> Result<Pin<Box<dyn Stream<Item = Result<ChatChunk, LlmError>> + Send>>, LlmError>

crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.name: build_messages
crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.doc_comment:
Convert arawn messages to Anthropic format.
Anthropic uses "user" and "assistant" roles only in the messages array.
System prompt is sent separately at the top level.
Tool results use role "user" with tool_result content blocks.

crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.name: merge_consecutive_roles
crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.doc_comment:
Merge consecutive messages with the same role into a single message
with array content blocks. Anthropic requires alternating roles.

crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.name: normalize_content
crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.doc_comment: Normalize content to a Vec<Value> of content blocks.

crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.name: build_tools
crates/arawn-llm/src/anthropic.rs.doc_comment: Convert tool definitions to Anthropic format.

crates/arawn-llm/src/client.rs.name: LlmClient
crates/arawn-llm/src/client.rs.doc_comment:
Provider-agnostic LLM client trait.
The engine codes against this trait, never a concrete provider.

crates/arawn-llm/src/client.rs.name: warmup
crates/arawn-llm/src/client.rs.doc_comment:
Probe a model with a minimal request to confirm it is reachable and
accepting traffic. Used both eagerly at startup and lazily before
real requests when the cached warmup state is stale.

The default impl issues a single 1-token chat completion via `stream`.
Provider implementations rarely need to override this.

crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.name: LlmError
crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.signature: Api | Auth | ModelNotFound | RateLimited | ServerError | Stream | Config | Request | Json

crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.name: is_retryable
crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Returns true if this error is transient and the request should be retried.

crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.name: from_status
crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Create from an HTTP status code + body.

crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.name: user_message
crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.doc_comment: Return a user-facing error message with actionable guidance.

crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.name: extract_api_message
crates/arawn-llm/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Try to extract a clean message from a JSON error body.
Groq/OpenAI format: {"error": {"message": "...", "type": "..."}}

crates/arawn-llm/src/mock.rs.doc_comment:
Mock LLM client that returns pre-scripted responses.
Each call to `stream()` consumes the next scripted response.
Panics if more calls are made than responses available.

crates/arawn-llm/src/warming.rs.name: DEFAULT_WARMUP_TTL
crates/arawn-llm/src/warming.rs.doc_comment:
Default TTL chosen for Ollama Cloud, which unloads idle models aggressively.
Local Ollama or Groq could safely use a much higher TTL, but a single
conservative default avoids per-provider config until we have evidence
it matters.

crates/arawn-llm/src/warming.rs.name: WarmingClient
crates/arawn-llm/src/warming.rs.signature: { inner: Arc<dyn LlmClient>, provider: String, ttl: Duration, last_warmup: Mutex<Option<Instant>> }
crates/arawn-llm/src/warming.rs.doc_comment:
Wraps any [`LlmClient`] with TTL-based warmup caching and a one-shot
retry-after-warmup on cold-restart-shaped errors.

crates/arawn-llm/src/warming.rs.name: WarmingClient
crates/arawn-llm/src/warming.rs.doc_comment:
Pool layering: raw provider → `RetryClient` → `WarmingClient`.

signature consistent with the model being unloaded by the provider.
after the cached warmup goes stale, and once after a request fails with a
configured model with a 1-token request the first time `stream` is called,
`WarmingClient` sits above `RetryClient` in the client stack. It probes the

Wrapper that caches model warmup state and re-warms on TTL expiry or cold-restart errors.

crates/arawn-memory/src/shortcodes.rs.name: apply_shortcodes
crates/arawn-memory/src/shortcodes.rs.doc_comment:
Scan text for repeated entity-like names and replace with shortcodes.

Names appearing `min_occurrences` or more times get a 2-3 char code
generated from first letters of each word. A legend is prepended.

Entity names are provided externally (from the KB) so we know exactly
which strings to look for, rather than guessing from the text.

crates/arawn-memory/src/shortcodes.rs.name: count_occurrences
crates/arawn-memory/src/shortcodes.rs.doc_comment: Count non-overlapping occurrences of needle in haystack.

crates/arawn-memory/src/shortcodes.rs.name: generate_code
crates/arawn-memory/src/shortcodes.rs.doc_comment:
Generate a shortcode from a name: first letter of each word, uppercased.
"arawn-engine" -> "AE", "Dylan Storey" -> "DS", "memory" -> "M"

crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.name: estimate_tokens
crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.doc_comment: Estimate token count from text length (matches arawn-engine's TokenEstimator).

crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.name: MemoryStack
crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.doc_comment: Layered memory stack. Call `wake_up()` per-message to get fresh L0+L1 context.

crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.name: new
crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.doc_comment:
L2: On-demand — topic-triggered retrieval (separate method)
L1: Essential facts (~500-800 tokens) — top-ranked entities grouped by type
L0: Identity (~100 tokens) — workstream metadata, people, conventions

Layered memory stack — generates token-budgeted context from the KB.

crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.name: wake_up
crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.doc_comment:
Generate L0 + L1 memory context within the given token budget.
Returns formatted text ready for system prompt injection.

crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.name: render_l0
crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.doc_comment: L0: Identity layer — workstream name + Person/Convention entities.

crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.name: render_l1_with_names
crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.doc_comment:
L1: Essential story — top-ranked entities grouped by type, within budget.
Returns (rendered text, entity titles included) for shortcode compression.

crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.name: l1_entity_titles
crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.doc_comment: Get the entity titles included in L1 (for L2 deduplication).

crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.name: topical_context
crates/arawn-memory/src/stack.rs.doc_comment:
L2: Topic-triggered context. Searches KB for entities matching keywords,
deduplicates against L1, returns formatted section within budget.

crates/arawn-memory/tests/longmemeval_be.name: reciprocal_rank_fusion
crates/arawn-memory/tests/longmemeval_be.doc_comment:
Reciprocal Rank Fusion: merge multiple ranked lists into one.
score(doc) = sum over lists: 1 / (k + rank_in_list)
k=60 is standard (Cormack et al. 2009).

crates/arawn-memory/tests/longmemeval_be.name: parse_date_to_days

crates/arawn-memory/tests/longmemeval_be.name: temporal_score
crates/arawn-memory/tests/longmemeval_be.doc_comment:
Temporal proximity score: higher for sessions closer in time to the question.
Returns a multiplier in [0.5, 1.5] — recent sessions get boosted, old ones dampened.

Resolve the sandbox root for a session.
Scratch: workstreams/scratch/<session-uuid>/

crates/arawn-tool/src/context.rs.name: resolve_llm
crates/arawn-tool/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve an [`LlmPreference`] against the runtime's LLM pool. Returns
`None` if no resolver is wired (e.g., test contexts). Tools that
declare an `llm_preference()` typically call this from inside
`execute()` and use the resolved client + match quality to decide
whether to proceed normally or degrade.

crates/arawn-tool/src/error.rs.name: ToolError
crates/arawn-tool/src/error.rs.doc_comment:
Errors that tools can return from `execute()`.

This is a simplified error type scoped to tool-level concerns.
The engine converts between `ToolError` and its own `EngineError` at the boundary.

crates/arawn-tool/src/llm_preference.rs.name: LlmPreference

crates/arawn-tool/src/llm_preference.rs.doc_comment:
What a tool or agent wants from an LLM.

Every field is optional. An empty preference (`LlmPreference::any()`) only
matches the engine fallback. The most specific field wins:
`named` > `provider` + `model` > `capabilities`.

crates/arawn-tool/src/llm_preference.rs.doc_comment:

`LlmClientPool` and attached to a context; the engine calls it via

object of a one-impl trait) so no new abstraction layer is introduced
just to wire the pool through.

crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.name: ToolRegistry
crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.signature: { tools: RwLock<HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Tool>>>, plugin_tools: RwLock<HashSet<String>> }
crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.doc_comment:
Registry of available tools. Supports hot-reload via register/unregister at runtime.
The engine queries this fresh each turn so changes take effect immediately.

crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.name: register_plugin
crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.doc_comment: Register a plugin-provided tool (tracked for hot-reload).

crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.name: register_arc
crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.doc_comment: Register an already-Arc'd tool (used when building filtered registries).

crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.name: plugin_tool_names
crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.doc_comment: Returns the names of all currently loaded plugin tools.

crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.name: get
crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.doc_comment: Get a tool by name. Returns a cloned Arc — no lock held after return.

crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.name: unregister_by_prefix
crates/arawn-tool/src/registry.rs.doc_comment: Unregister all tools whose names start with the given prefix.

crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.name: ToolCategory
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.signature: Core | Task | Agent | Web | Memory | Plan | Workstream | Utility | BackgroundTask
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.doc_comment:
Category of a tool — used for permission checking, context filtering, and
tool grouping.

crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.name: PermissionCategory
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.doc_comment:
Risk class of a tool — used by the permission system to decide fallback
behaviour when no explicit rule matches. Orthogonal to [`ToolCategory`]
(which groups tools by feature area for context filtering).

crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.name: Tool
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.signature: { fn name(), fn description(), fn parameters_schema(), fn execute(), fn is_read_only(), fn category(), fn permission_cat...
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.doc_comment: A tool that can be invoked by the LLM.

crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.name: is_read_only
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.doc_comment: Whether this tool is side-effect-free (observation only).

crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.name: category
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.doc_comment: Tool category for context filtering and feature-area grouping.

crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.name: permission_category
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.doc_comment:
Permission risk class for permission-mode fallback decisions.
Default is `Other` (conservative — requires explicit allow).
Read-only tools should return `ReadOnly` so they auto-allow in
Default mode and are permitted in Plan mode. File-writing and shell
tools should return their matching variant.

crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.name: llm_preference
crates/arawn-tool/src/tool.rs.doc_comment:
Optional preferred LLM for this tool. The engine resolves this against
the context's resolver before calling [`Tool::execute`] and makes
the resolved client available via [`ToolContext::preferred_llm`].
Defaults to `None` — most tools don't need an LLM at all.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment:

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: rendered_lines
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment:
Get or compute the cached markdown rendering for assistant messages.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: App

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: All mutable TUI state. Pure state machine — no I/O, no network.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: handle_action
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Process an action and mutate state. Returns true if state changed.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: update_autocomplete
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Update autocomplete suggestions based on current input buffer.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: accept_autocomplete
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Accept the currently selected autocomplete suggestion.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: apply_engine_event
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Apply a streaming engine event to the app state (testable without network).

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: load_session_messages
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment:
Load messages from a session detail JSON response into the chat.
Clears existing messages and streaming text first.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: format_tool_input
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Format tool input args into a compact display string.

crates/arawn-tui/src/command.rs.doc_comment:
- **Skill**: /skill-name — invoke a user-invocable skill via the server
- **Inventory**: /plugins, /skills, /agents, /mcp, /tools — query server
- **Built-in**: /help, /clear, /plan — handled client-side
three flavors:
Commands are detected by a "/" prefix in the input buffer. They come in

crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.doc_comment: Extract text from a rectangular region of the buffer.

crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.name: chat_region_for
crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.doc_comment:
Extract the chat area text. When sidebar is visible (focus == Sidebar),
chat is right 80%; otherwise chat is full width.

crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.name: chat_region
crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.doc_comment: Convenience: chat region for default app (sidebar hidden).

crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.name: sidebar_region
crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.doc_comment:
Extract the sidebar text (left 20%, rows 1..height-3).
Only meaningful when sidebar is visible (focus == Sidebar).

crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.name: input_region
crates/arawn-tui/src/render.rs.doc_comment: Extract the input bar text (second from bottom row).

crates/arawn-tui/src/snapshot.rs.name: buffer_to_snapshot
crates/arawn-tui/src/snapshot.rs.doc_comment:
Render a TestBackend buffer to a deterministic string for snapshot comparison.
Text only — no style information. Good for layout verification.

crates/arawn-tui/src/snapshot.rs.name: buffer_to_styled_snapshot
crates/arawn-tui/src/snapshot.rs.doc_comment:
Render a TestBackend buffer with inline style annotations.
Style changes are marked with `«fg:Color,bg:Color,mod»` tags.
Only emits a tag when the style changes from the previous cell.
This captures colors and modifiers alongside the text content.

crates/arawn/src/llm_pool.rs.name: warmup_all
crates/arawn/src/llm_pool.rs.doc_comment:
Warm up every entry concurrently. Returns a vector of `(name, result)`
so callers can log per-entry outcomes. Never fails as a whole — a bad
model surfaces as `Err` for that entry while others may still succeed.

crates/arawn/src/llm_pool.rs.name: resolve
crates/arawn/src/llm_pool.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve an [`LlmPreference`] against the pool. Always succeeds — the
engine LLM is the unconditional fallback.

Resolution order:
1. Named match — `preference.named` is set and exists in the pool → `Exact`.
2. Provider+model match — both fields set and an entry matches → `Exact`.
3. Capability match — first entry satisfying `preference.capabilities` → `Capability`.
4. Fallback — engine default → `Fallback`.

(

crates/arawn/src/main.rs.doc_comment: Register all default tools into the registry.

crates/arawn/src/main.rs.name: connect_mcp_servers
crates/arawn/src/main.rs.doc_comment: Connect to MCP servers from config and plugins.

crates/arawn/src/plugin_cmd.rs.name: parse_scope
crates/arawn/src/plugin_cmd.rs.doc_comment: Parse --scope flag from args. Defaults to User.

crates/arawn/src/plugin_cmd.rs.name: parse_marketplace_source
crates/arawn/src/plugin_cmd.rs.doc_comment:
Parse a marketplace source string.

- `org/repo` → GitHub source
- `https://...` → Git URL source
- `/local/path` → Directory source

crates/arawn/src/plugin_cmd.rs.signature:
(
    plugins_root: &Path,
    identifier: &str,

) -> Result<(), String>
crates/arawn/src/plugin_cmd.rs.doc_comment: Update enabledPlugins in settings.json at the plugins root.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: PROTOCOL_VERSION
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment: Protocol version reported by the `hello` handshake.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: Request
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.signature: { id: u64, method: String, params: Value }

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: Response
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.signature: { id: u64, result: Option<Value>, error: Option<ErrorBody> }

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: ErrorBody
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.signature: { code: String, message: String, details: Option<Value> }

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: from_service_error
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment:
Build an error response from a [`ServiceError`]. Preserves the
stable code, the display message, and — for variants that wrap
typed sources — a structured `details` object with the inner
error's `kind` so clients can dispatch on it.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: AppState
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment: Shared app state for the WebSocket server.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: generate_auth_token
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment: Generate a random auth token for WebSocket connections.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: write_token_file
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment: Write the auth token to {data_dir}/server.token for clients to read.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: read_token_file
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment:
Read the auth token from {data_dir}/server.token.
Falls back to ARAWN_DATA_DIR env var, then ~/.arawn.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: run_server
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment: Start the WebSocket server on the given port.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: shutdown_signal
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment: Wait for a shutdown signal (Ctrl-C / SIGTERM).

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: decision_handler
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.signature:
(
    State(AppState { service, .. }): State<AppState>,
    Json(req): Json<arawn_workflow::agent_executor::DecisionRequest>,
) -> impl IntoResponse
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment:
HTTP endpoint for workflow decision tasks.
Decision tasks in cloacina pipelines call POST /api/decision to run
the QueryEngine and get agent-powered responses.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: ws_handler
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.signature:
(
    ws: WebSocketUpgrade,
    Query(params): Query<WsQueryParams>,
    State(state): State<AppState>,
) -> impl IntoResponse

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: handle_connection_public
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment: Handle a single WebSocket connection. Public for integration tests.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: from_service_error_preserves_structured_detail_for_typed_variants
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment:
Typed Storage error should round-trip through the wire payload with
structured `details` (kind tag) alongside the code + message.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: from_service_error_omits_details_for_string_only_variants
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment:
String-only variants (NotFound, InvalidOperation, Internal) keep
code + message but have no `details` payload — the serialized wire
form must omit the field entirely, not emit null.

crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.name: from_service_error_preserves_engine_error_kind
crates/arawn/src/ws_server.rs.doc_comment:
Engine errors surface a `kind` that identifies the inner variant —
tool_not_found here — so clients can dispatch without string-parsing
the message.
